exercises.title: Inspect the Process Tree
exercises.description: Use ps and pstree to find your shell, its parent, and PID 1.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can identify your shell and explain the parent-child relationships in the tree.

exercises.title: Create a Zombie
exercises.description: Write a short script that forks a child, does not wait, and observe the zombie state.
exercises.expected_outcome: You should see a zombie child in ps until the parent reaps it.

exercises.title: Fork and Exec
exercises.description: Write a small C program that forks and runs ls -la in the child, then checks the exit code.
exercises.expected_outcome: The child should replace itself with ls and the parent should print the exit status.

exercises.title: Mini Shell
exercises.description: Build a simple command loop that forks, execs, and waits without pipes or redirection.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can run basic commands interactively and exit cleanly.

exercises.title: Process Pool
exercises.description: Implement N worker processes that consume jobs from a queue and shut down on SIGTERM.
exercises.expected_outcome: Workers process tasks concurrently and exit gracefully on termination.

exercises.title: Syscall Tracer
exercises.description: Use ptrace to log the syscalls made by a child process.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can see syscall numbers or names as the child runs.
